person.name: Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children )
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
SIR ROBERT COLLIER ws born about 1453 of Pas-de-Calais, France, to Robert Hugh Collier (1425-1487) and Agnes Venables (1433-1486.)  He married Isabella Doddington about 1483 of England.

Sir Robert Collier died about 1522 of Stone, Staffordshire, England, age 69.

ROBERT COLEIRE de DARLASTON (c.1453-c.1505) Robert Coleire was born in France about 1453 and died in Staffordshire, England about 1505. He married Isabella Doddington, daughter of Sir John He married Isabella Doddington, daughter of Sir John Doddington, about 1483. Isabella was born in 1460 in Stafford, England.[1]
ROBERT COLEIRE de DARLASTON (c.1453- c.1505)
Robert Coleire was born in France about 1453 and died in Staffordshire, England about 1505. He married lsabella Doddington, daughter of Sir John Doddington, about 1483. Robert Colleire came to England about 1482, long preceeding the arrival of other members of his family in England and Ireland.
He settled in the market town of Stone Manor, in Straffordshire County, England. The town stands on the river Trent and Trent Mersey Canal, 7 miles north nortwest of Strafford, 7 miles south of Stoke-upon-Trent, and 137 miles from London.
He was a taylor (tailor), draper (a dealer in cloth or in (clothes), then a woolbuyer. Some of these staplers (dealers in staple goods) grew to great wealth. In the year 1503, two years before his death, Robert and Isabella moved to Darlaston Manor.
Robert and his son Thurston leased Darlaston Manor, in the county of Staffordshire, from Thomas Whally, then in 1537, Robert's son, James purchased the manor from Richard Whalley.

Robert Coleire was born in France about 1453 and died in Straffordshire, England about 1505. He married lsabella Doddington, daughter of Sir John Doddington, about 1483.

Robert Coleire came to England about 1482, long preceding the arrival of other members of his family in England and Ireland. He settled in the market town of Stone Manor, in Straffordshire County, England. The town stands on the river Trent and Trent Mersey Canal, 7 miles north northwest of Strafford, 7 miles south of Stoke-upon-Trent,and 137 miles from London. He was a taylor (tailor), draper (a dealer in cloth or clothes), then a wool buyer. Some of these staplers (dealers in staple goods) grew to great wealth. In the year 1503, two years before his death, Robert and Isabella moved to Darlaston Manor. Robert and his son Thurston leased Darlaston Manor, in the county of Straffordshire, from Thomas Whally, and then in 1537, Robert's son James purchased the manor from Richard Whally.

Except for a brief time when Robert's great grandson, James Collier, sold the manor to his father-in-law in 1597 and until his son Francis repurchased it from his grandfather in 1597, there was a Collier (Coleire) as Lord of Darlaston for over 180 years, until a great-great-great-great-grandson, James Collier (Coleire) sold it to William Jervis in 1685.
Robert Coliere de Darlaston: Robert Coliére de Darlaston was a contemporary of Christopher Columbus. Both may have been in the service of Queen Isabella and King Ferdinand at the same time. Both started a move to America at about the same time. Columbus reached America first, but Robert's move was more lasting. Prior to the reign of Ferdinand and Isabella, Spain consisted of many little Christian kingdoms, all engaged in the re-conquest of Spain from the Moors. Castile finally became the most powerful and in the 13th century managed to confine the moors to a small area near Granada. The kings of Spain needed the support of the middle class in this struggle and representatives of the towns were admitted to the national council. When Isabella, the young Queen of Castile, and Ferdinand, King of Aragon, fell in love and married they laid the foundation of a united Spain and together they conquered Granada. A voluntary association of the towns and the rural gentry to clear the country of brigands was established in 1475 , with 7-year memberships for foreigners. This brotherhood or fraternity, called the Hermandad consisted of horsemen in proportion of one for every 100 families. In the war with Granada, the Hermandad was employed as soldiers. Warriors from all over Europe swarmed into Spain to help in this war. It is quite possible that Robert Coliére, as a foreign legionnaire was among the foreigners, including Englishmen, who were welcomed to this service in 1475. In 1482, the Moorish Boabdil, deposed his father, who fled to Malaga, but the advance of the forces of Ferdinand and Isabella forced him to resign the task of defense into more war-like hands in 1483. It is not unreasonable to speculate that, at the end of a 7-year contract, young Robert Coliére might have been induced to accompany an English friend to England. There in Staffordshire, as a young, former officer, he would have been introduced into good circles and thus met and married Sir John Doddington's daughter Isabella. Robert Coliére's arrival in England about 1482, long preceding the arrival of other members of this family in England and Ireland. He settled in the market town of Stone, in Staffordshire County, England. The town stands on the river Trent and the Trent and Mersey Canal, 7 miles north northwest of Stafford, 7 miles south of Stoke-upon-Trent, and 137 miles from London. Robert was first a taylor (tailor), then a draper (a dealer in cloth or in clothes), and then a woolbuyer. Some of these staplers (dealers in staple goods) grew to great wealth. In the year 1503, two years before his death, Robert and Isabella moved to Darlaston Manor. Robert and his son Thurston leased Darlaston Manor, in the county of Staffordshire, from Thomas Whalley, then in 1537, Robert's son, James, purchased the manor from Richard Whalley. Except for a brief time when Robert's great grandson, James Collier, sold the manor to his father-in-law in 1597 and until James's son, Francis, repurchased it from his grandfather in 1597, there was a Coliére as Lord of Darlaston for over 180 years. A great-great-great-great- grandson, James Coliére sold Darlaston to William Jervis in 1685. Robert Coliére de Darlaston's great-great-great-grandson Isaac Collyer/Collier Sr. emigrated to America between 1653 and 1670, settling in York Co., Virginia. Isaac was my wife's G-G-G-G-G- G-Grandfather. The details I have are sketchy and there are a few improbable dates. I would also like to contact researchers of the English Colliers. Vaughn Ballard [vballard@airmail.net] 8/7/99

ROBERT COLEIRE de DARLASTON (c.1453-c.1505) Robert Coleire was born in France about 1453 and died in Staffordshire, England about 1505. He married Isabella Doddington, daughter of Sir John He married Isabella Doddington, daughter of Sir John Doddington, about 1483. Isabella was born in 1460 in Stafford, England.[1]

ISABELLA DODDINGTON was born about 1460 of Staffordshire, England, to John Doddington (1445-1476) and Elizabeth Hussey (1432-1462.)  She married Robert Collier about 1483 of England.

Isabella Doddington died 30 March 1548, England, age 88.

 
Dottington estate

	Children of Robert Collier and Isabella Doddington:

	1.	Margery Collier (1483-1522)
	2.	Thurston Collier (1486-1522)
	3.	*JAMES COLLIER (1490-1548)
	4.	Joyce Collier (1494-)
	5.	George Cauliere (1495-1558)
	6.	Robert Coliere (1498-1522)

nameMeanings.name: Doddington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from any of several places called Doddington (Cambs, Kent, Lincs, Cheshire, Northumb) or Dodington (Gloucs, Somerset, Shrops), or from Dry Doddington (Lincs), Great Doddington (Northants). In addition, Dunton (Bucks), Denton (Northants), Derrington (Staffs), and Detton Hall (Shrops) all appear in Domesday Book as Dodintone, and may have contributed to the surname.

variants.name: Domesday Book
meanings: English: locative name from either of two places in Lancs, namely Bispham near Blackpool ( Bischopham, 1086 in Domesday Book ) or Bispham in Croston.

nameMeanings.name: Isabella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

en.lifeSummary: Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) was born in 1460 as the daughter of Sir John Doddington and Elizabeth Hughes. She married Robert Collier about 1483, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 30 March 1548, at the age of 88.

fr.lifeSummary: Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) est née en 1460, fille de Sir John Doddington et Elizabeth Hughes. Elle a épousé Robert Collier environ 1483, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 30 mars 1548, à l’âge de 88 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) wurde 1460, als Tochter von Sir John Doddington und Elizabeth Hughes geboren. Sie hat um 1483 in England, United Kingdom Robert Collier geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 30. März 1548 im Alter von 88 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) è nata il nel 1460 come figlia di Sir John Doddington e Elizabeth Hughes. Lei sposato Robert Collier circa 1483, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 30 marzo 1548, all'età di 88 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children )(은)는 1460년 Sir John Doddington(와)과 Elizabeth Hughes의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1483년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Robert Collier(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1548년 3월 30일 88세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) nasceu em 1460 filha de Sir John Doddington e Elizabeth Hughes. Ela casou-se com Robert Collier aproximadamente 1483, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 30 de março de 1548, com 88 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) родилась в 1460 г. Её родителей звали Sir John Doddington и Elizabeth Hughes. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Collier около 1483 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 30 марта 1548 г. в возрасте 88 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Isabella Doddington ( 6 Children ) nació en 1460 como hija de Sir John Doddington y Elizabeth Hughes. Se casó con Robert Collier aproximadamente 1483, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 30 de marzo de 1548, a la edad de 88 años.
